description: Active Directory penetration testing exam focusing on LDAP enumeration, Kerberos roasting, credential abuse, BloodHound-style reasoning, and lateral movement techniques.

scenarios.description: Windows Active Directory domain with 150+ users, multiple service accounts, and misconfigured permissions
objectives: Perform Kerberoasting attack to extract service account hashes
Enumerate SMB shares with valid credentials
Extract domain user flag and Domain Admin flag

hints.strategic: Active Directory attacks follow a pattern: enumerate → extract → crack → lateral movement → domain admin. Start with LDAP enumeration to map the domain structure.
hints.tactical: Service accounts with SPNs are Kerberoastable. Look for accounts in common groups: Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, Account Operators. BloodHound can visualize attack paths.
hints.technical: Impacket toolkit is essential: GetUserSPNs.py for Kerberoasting, secretsdump.py for credential extraction, psexec.py for lateral movement. CrackMapExec (or NetExec) is the Swiss Army knife of AD pentesting.
hints.specific: Default LDAP queries: ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.10.250 -b 'DC=corp,DC=thm'. For Kerberoasting: GetUserSPNs.py corp.thm/user:password -request -outputfile hashes.txt

ldapEnumeration: bloodyAD -u user -p password -d corp.thm --host 10.10.10.250 get object 'Domain Users'
bloodHoundEnumeration: Import into BloodHound GUI for path analysis

commandCommentary.ldapsearch: Standard LDAP query tool. Flags: -x (simple auth), -H (LDAP URI), -b (base DN), -D (bind DN), -w (password). Essential for manual AD enumeration.
commandCommentary.GetUserSPNs.py: Impacket tool for Kerberoasting. Extracts TGS tickets for service accounts. Output can be cracked with hashcat/john. Requires valid domain credentials.
commandCommentary.crackmapexec: Swiss Army knife of AD pentesting. Supports SMB, LDAP, WinRM, MSSQL, RDP protocols. Flags: -u (username), -p (password), -H (NTLM hash), --shares, --sam, --lsa.
commandCommentary.nxc: NetExec - modern fork of CrackMapExec with additional features. Same syntax as crackmapexec. Recommended for Kali 2024+. Supports multiple protocols and database storage.
commandCommentary.psexec.py: Impacket tool for remote command execution. Requires admin credentials or NTLM hash. Creates service on target - can be detected by EDR.
commandCommentary.secretsdump.py: Impacket tool for credential extraction. Dumps SAM, LSA secrets, cached credentials, NTDS.dit. Requires admin privileges on target.
commandCommentary.hashcat: GPU-accelerated password cracker. Mode 13100 for Kerberos TGS-REP. Mode 1000 for NTLM. Much faster than john on systems with GPU.
commandCommentary.bloodhound: Graph-based AD relationship mapper. Identifies shortest path to Domain Admin. Collector: bloodhound-python (Python) or SharpHound (C#). GUI for analysis.

phase1.name: Initial Access & Enumeration
steps: Obtain valid domain credentials (provided or via password spray)
Identify privileged groups and their members

phase2.name: Kerberoasting & Credential Abuse
steps: Extract TGS tickets for service accounts using GetUserSPNs.py
Crack service account hashes offline with hashcat
Test cracked credentials against SMB shares
Identify high-value targets for lateral movement

phase3.name: Lateral Movement
steps: Use valid credentials to access workstations/servers

phase4.name: Domain Dominance
steps: Obtain Domain Admin credentials or hash
Capture both user and Domain Admin flags
